title: nie-bot: notification dispatcher tests
description:
What: Unit test suite for bot/src/dispatcher.rs.

Algorithm (tests to write, all in #[cfg(test)] in dispatcher.rs):
1. test_dispatch_deliver_chat: construct JsonRpcNotification with method=deliver, params={from:pub_id, payload:base64(serde_json::to_vec(ClearMessage::Chat{text:"hello"}))}, assert dispatch returns BotEvent::MessageReceived{text:"hello"}
   Oracle: hardcode expected JSON bytes independently, not from ClearMessage::to_vec
2. test_dispatch_user_joined: method=user_joined, params={pub_id,nickname,sequence}, assert BotEvent::UserJoined
3. test_dispatch_user_left: assert BotEvent::UserLeft
4. test_dispatch_unknown_method: method=unknown_xyz, assert Ok(None)
5. test_dispatch_malformed_payload: method=deliver, payload=invalid_base64_garbage, assert Ok(None) (not Err)
6. test_dispatch_missing_params: method=deliver, params=null, assert Ok(None) (not panic)

Defensive: Expected payload bytes are computed using an INDEPENDENT encoding (not the bot dispatcher code itself).

Agent team: T (tester — oracle is the JSON-RPC spec and protocol.rs constants).
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-bot bot::dispatcher passes; all 6 cases present; malformed input never panics
